metadata.name: Mount NFS File Systems
metadata.description: Manually and persistently mount NFS export directories
objectives: Manually mount the NFS export directory
Mount the NFS export directory persistently

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: NFS client mounting shares from serverb

tasks.name: Verify /public mount point exists
tasks.description: The /public directory must exist as the mount point for the NFS share

tasks.name: Verify NFS entry in /etc/fstab
tasks.description: The /etc/fstab file must contain an entry for the NFS mount
tasks.failure_message: The NFS entry is missing or incorrect in /etc/fstab

tasks.name: Verify NFS share is mounted on /public
tasks.description: The serverb:/shares/public NFS share should be mounted on /public
tasks.success_message: The NFS share is mounted on /public
tasks.failure_message: The NFS share is not mounted on /public
hints: Or manually: sudo mount -t nfs serverb:/shares/public /public

tasks.name: Verify NFS content is accessible
tasks.description: The hello file from the NFS export should be readable

tasks.name: Verify mount persists after reload
tasks.description: The mount should be configured to persist across reboots via /etc/fstab
tasks.success_message: The mount is configured to persist across reboots
tasks.failure_message: The mount is not configured to persist across reboots
hints: Test mount from fstab: sudo umount /public && sudo mount /public
